item.title: It's depressing.
item.description: I like a nice range of news and memes on Imgur, but every other week the USA's dystopia flares up, and it's extremely depressing to even think about. The USA is like a non-stop head-shaking machine, even when there isn't a mass shooting, there's the insane politicians, or some disgusting story about a kid who died because of an insane medical system, or a story of someone who went to jail because of the insane legal system, or some infuriating stat about horrible education, or news that lawmakers have successfully restricted freedoms in line with ISIS. It's so depressing that so many citizens have such garbage support, education, and living conditions, to the point they are horribly disadvantaged by policy that that effectively can't change because they don't know any better, exacerbated by a garbage political and voting system. If the average US citizen knew what they were missing out on, I'm sure things would change, but of course there's the world-wide Murdoch plague that ensures that never happens. I would love to help, but the only thing that I think can work is education, and this post is about the limit of my power. *Front page edit* Subscribe to me on YouTube or whatever.
item.dc:creator: KoryNunn

item.title: 3rd Cakeday meme dump, stole these, now I'm giving them back
item.description:
I once saw a video of a frog doing this after having eaten a moth.
I have osmosed enough of TCW to know this is funny (please don't tell me to watch it, I'm not going to, the animation style is ugly as hell and there's like nine million hours of it, I can't make myself look at something I don't like looking at for that long)
Sometimes the lil pet is just another party member, and that's fine too.
I worked in a call center for a decade, this happened a LOT
Saw the meme text on a different canon and knew what I had to do...
GNU uncle Pete
we've all got a picture like this in a family album, right?
Still want to make this into a cross-stitch sampler
Another cartoon I haven't seen, though I intend to watch this one when the neurodivergence department says I can. I just enjoy this dude as a person.
hashtag Just Eliot Spencer Things
And other things I hope I get to hear Oscar Isaac say
Pretty sure that is word for word the response I would get if I corduroyed my father.
GNU Carrie Fisher
See also: Quicksand
My favorite part of this is how dead serious the cat looks, it's gonna heck somebody up good.
Seriously though, where can I sign up?
♪If only, if only...♪
This seems both pointless and expensive, but I still want one
Amphibian tax as unfortunately my kitty passed last October
funny turtle tax
item.dc:creator: SterlingBee

item.dc:creator: imgurnaknockuout

item.title: Top 7 Best News of Last Week — May 29, 2022
item.description:
1. Church of Scotland to allow same-sex marriages - The Church of Scotland has voted to allow clergy to conduct same-sex marriages for the first time. It means same-sex couples will be able to marry in church in services conducted by ministers.
2. Cosmetic surgery adverts targeting teenagers banned - Adverts for cosmetic procedures are now banned from targeting under-18s in the UK. The new rules apply to all media and cover procedures that are designed to change a person's physical appearance - including breast augmentation or reduction, "tummy tucks", eyelid surgery, nose reshaping, and facelifts.
3. Spanish parliament approves 'only yes means yes' consent bill - This is really important, because sexual assault survivors will no longer have to prove violence was used against them. The new bill defines consent as an explicit expression of a person's will, making it clear that silence or passivity do not equal consent.
4. Oklahoma City Zoo announces pregnancy of critically endangered Sumatran tiger - The Oklahoma City Zoo announced that its female Sumatran tiger, Lola, is pregnant and due to give birth this summer.
5. Spanish diver saves 12-metre humpback whale in 'daring' rescue mission - The whale was stuck in a fishing net. Watch the full video of the rescue: https://youtu.be/tV4MDsIgs_8
6. A video from Florida Fish and Wildlife shows a panther kitten being reunited with her mom. - The panther came to check out the kitten cries, but couldn't rescue it. Good human opened the cage off screen. Mom panther stuck around long enough to let kitten wander out. Then mom panther and kitten wandered off into the night. Very sweet!! https://youtu.be/DM_ARppRzS8
7. Wild mammals making comeback in Europe - the continent's mammal populations are flourishing again thanks to conservation programs and more productive agriculture.
That's it for this week :) Until next week, stay safe dont forget to share this with your friends. You can read the previous posts and sources here https://feelgoodnews.substack.com/
item.dc:creator: FeelGoodNews

letting the days go by

item.title: Slavery exists for the poor in the school to prison pipeline. Rich white kids get off from “affluenza” while black & brown children as you g as 4 get handcuffed and arrested for stuff white abled bodied children get a timeout for
item.description: Slavery exists for the poor in the school to prison pipeline. Rich white kids get off from “affluenza” while black & brown children as young as 4 get handcuffed and arrested for stuff white abled bodied children get a timeout for https://www.aclu.org/news/criminal-law-reform/police-in-schools-continue-to-target-black-brown-and-indigenous-students-with-disabilities-the-trump-administration-has-data-thats-likely-to-prove-it this is actual critical race theory and its parent critical legal studies and as applied to the legislation and law between rich and poor and how it inherited is used for eugenics to make it worse for BIPOC and disabled people https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Critical_race_theory https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Critical_legal_studies https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Disability_studies
item.dc:creator: roanhouse

item.title: May the Force be with you (because we won't)
item.description: Most Viral Edit: America is a hellscape that can only change by voting out the GOP everywhere they live. Democrats may not be effective politicians but they also aren't actively trying to destroy the concept of democracy. And if your complaint is that they're too centrist or don't go far enough, if you don't think incrementalism works, look at Republicans. They've spent over 40 years reaching this point, one baby step right at a time.
item.dc:creator: TheNextPresident
